focus: correctness: lifecycle state, dependency ordering, implementation-summary truthfulness, and runtime-claim support
findingsSummary: Two P1 correctness findings: stale continuation/progress metadata for completed research and omitted machine-readable implementation dependencies.

findingDetails.title: Completed research phases still advertise pre-dispatch continuation state
findingDetails.scopeProof: Exact lifecycle search covered parent and all ten child phases; contradictions confirmed against current implementation summaries.
findingDetails.claim: Continuation fields direct consumers to repeat completed research and report stale completion.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Implementation summaries and status rows correctly report completion and contain impact for consumers that always prefer them.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: Spec frontmatter may be a non-authoritative historical snapshot after an implementation summary exists.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Prove every resume, indexing, and status consumer ignores spec and parent continuity after an implementation summary exists.

findingDetails.title: Dependency graph metadata omits every declared implementation prerequisite
findingDetails.scopeProof: Exact metadata search found empty depends_on arrays for all ten children; direct reads confirmed disagreement in four dependency-bearing phases.
findingDetails.claim: Graph metadata cannot represent implementation build order because declared prerequisites are absent.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Parent and child prose preserve human-readable order, but no alternate graph dependency field was found.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: Manual arrays may require a separate explicit authoring step rather than prose derivation.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Prove graph/resume consumers do not use manual.depends_on and identify another canonical machine-readable dependency source.

traceabilityChecks.evidence: Lifecycle and planned-runtime claims checked; consumer-level contracts remain.

noveltyJustification: First iteration established two independent severity-weighted correctness defects; weightedNew=10, weightedRefinement=0, weightedTotal=10.
edgeCases: Implementation-summary precedence partially contains stale spec continuity.
Parent planned status is compatible with seven planned implementation phases.
Structural-impact analysis unavailable; direct graphless evidence used.
ruledOut: No false shipped-behavior finding; absent runtime engine agrees with planned summaries.
Per-style corpus review is out of scope.
integrationEvidence: Exact named corpus-contract searches under .opencode/skills/sk-design found no implementation surfaces

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: Complex phase-parent scope with ten children and named runtime integration surfaces.

searchLedger.result: Confirmed planned runtime absence is truthful.

focus: security: corpus-to-prompt and transport trust boundaries

findingsSummary.summary: Three P1 security planning-contract findings: missing hydration path containment, contradictory unknown-rights eligibility, and unspecified malicious-instruction handling for STUDY prompt material.

findingDetails.title: Hydration has no path-containment or symlink safety contract
findingDetails.scopeProof: Exact phase-004 path-safety search found no containment control; complete hydration and checklist sections were directly read.
findingDetails.claim: Manifest-selected artifact reads lack canonical-root and symlink containment requirements.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Searched all phase-004 canonical documents for realpath, symlink, traversal, allowlist, relative-path, and outside-root controls; reviewed generation hashing.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: Committed manifests and re-hashing reduce drift but do not prove resolved-path containment.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: An incorporated governing parser contract proves root containment and symlink rejection for every read.

findingDetails.title: Unknown-rights styles are both forbidden from results and permitted as decision references
findingDetails.scopeProof: Compared eligibility ADR, P0 requirement, security NFR, edge case, and checklist across ranking, cards, hydration, and proof consumers.
findingDetails.claim: Rights-unknown styles are simultaneously excluded from results and eligible as rationale references.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Reviewed exact-reuse limitation and proof-card gate as possible narrower interpretations.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: A reference-only state may be intended, but it is not typed and ADR-002 prohibits entry into results broadly.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Typed reference-only rights state, raw-hydration prohibition, reconciled ADR, and fixtures are specified.

findingDetails.title: The STUDY injection envelope has no malicious-instruction handling or test oracle
findingDetails.scopeProof: Exact phase-006 search found envelope/leak controls but no malicious, untrusted, sanitize, escape, or instruction-handling contract.
findingDetails.claim: STUDY does not define or test resistance to malicious corpus instructions.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Reviewed de-literalization, target-facts binding, malformed-envelope refusal, and all adversarial fixture requirements.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: Those controls reduce copying and authority drift but do not define handling of semantically preserved instructions.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: The contract treats source text as inert data, neutralizes instruction-like content, and adds seeded fail-closed prompt-injection fixtures.

traceabilityChecks.note: Security contracts mapped; prior consumer route not retried.
Checklist lines used as contract evidence only; blocked completion-evidence protocol not retried.

noveltyJustification: All three findings expose security contract classes not present in iteration 1; weightedNew=15 and weightedTotal=25, so ratio=0.60.
edgeCases: Findings concern planned contracts, not active runtime exploits.

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: Complex phase-parent security scope with seven planned children and named runtime integration surfaces.

findingDetails.claim: Canonical handoff/output pointers for completed research resolve to nonexistent unlineaged paths.

findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: Paths may predate lineage placement, but they occupy canonical Output/Verification fields and no aliases exist.

focus: maintainability: ownership, shared-contract duplication, concrete locations, sequencing, and rollback independence across phases 004-010

findingsSummary.summary: One P2 maintainability finding: phase 007 has no concrete shared-package owner or explicit mapping to phase 004 proof fields; P1-002 is refined with phases 009-010 dependency evidence.

findingDetails.title: The shared cross-mode contract has no concrete package owner or explicit phase-004 proof mapping
findingDetails.scopeProof: Exact ownership/proof searches and direct reads covered phase 004, phase 007, and all phase-008-010 consumers; phase 007 remains pathless and no adapter/import from CORPUS_USE_PROOF v1 is named.

findingDetails.title: Dependency graph metadata omits every declared implementation prerequisite
findingDetails.scopeProof: The prior all-child empty depends_on sweep is now paired with direct phase-009 and phase-010 dependency reads, covering the complete planned implementation chain.
findingDetails.claim: Graph metadata cannot represent the complete 004-010 implementation build order because declared prerequisites are absent from child dependency arrays.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Human-readable plans preserve ordering, including phases 009 and 010, but no alternate machine-readable dependency source is identified.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: Dependency arrays may require a later explicit metadata-authoring step rather than prose derivation.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 if graph/resume consumers do not use manual.depends_on and another canonical machine-readable dependency source is proven.

noveltyJustification: Weighted novelty is (1 new P2 + 0.5 x one refined P1=2.5) / 31 total active severity weight = 3.5/31; the new advisory isolates a distinct ownership boundary while the dependency evidence refines P1-002 instead of duplicating it.
edgeCases: Phase-004 and phase-007 proof contracts may intentionally be different layers; finding requests an explicit mapping decision, not forced unification.
Phase 006 is md-generator-specific and excluded from the phase-007 non-md-generator seam.
Structural-impact analysis unavailable; future transitive imports cannot be enumerated.

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: Seven planned phases share producer/consumer contracts, proof fields, dependencies, and rollback boundaries.

targetSelection.selectionReason: These files define the shared owners, producer/consumer boundaries, implementation locations, phase prerequisites, and rollback controls requested by the maintainability focus.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Overlapping proof fields can acquire parallel owners because the shared seam has no concrete package/import boundary or phase-004 mapping.

searchActions.result: Phase 004 names corpus-use-proof.mjs; phase 007 names only a proposed shared package and downstream consumers reference phase 007.
No package path, exported type owner, or mapping to CORPUS_USE_PROOF v1 is defined.

searchLedger.rationale: A concrete owner/mapping decision is required before five downstream consumers can import rather than copy the shared contract.

searchLedger.invariant: Every documented implementation prerequisite should have one machine-readable ordering representation.

searchActions.result: Phase 009 declares 004/007/008 dependencies and phase 010 declares 007/008, extending the same omission already recorded in P1-002.

searchLedger.rationale: The evidence broadens the existing class-of-bug through the terminal implementation phases and is a refinement, not a duplicate.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Downstream modes redefine phase-007 shared fields locally.

searchActions.result: Phase 008 binds without extending the schema; phase 009 marks the seam unchanged and consumed.

searchLedger.rationale: The planned consumer contracts explicitly prohibit local extension/redefinition.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Direct plan language requires reference/consumption of the phase-007 owner rather than copied fields.

searchLedger.hypothesis: A failed consumer cannot be disabled or reverted without unwinding shared predecessors or sibling consumers.

searchActions.result: Phase 006 has an isolated STUDY switch, phase 008 requires one flag per consumer, and phases 009/010 confine reverts to owned directories.

searchLedger.rationale: Each consumer phase has a local off/revert boundary that leaves shared phases intact.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Direct rollback contracts prove phase-local reversibility; phase 008 additionally requires per-consumer flags.

searchDebt: Future transitive imports cannot be enumerated until implementation exists and the code graph is available.

focus: cross-reference stabilization: replay active P1-001 through P1-006 and confirm P2-007 classification

findingsSummary.summary: All six active P1 findings and P2-007 remain active at their prior severity after current-file replay; no new or refined finding.

findingDetails.title: Completed research phases still advertise pre-dispatch continuation state
findingDetails.evidence: Current parent, phase-002, and phase-003 spec continuity remains stale against complete/100% implementation summaries.
findingDetails.recommendation: Reconcile canonical continuity fields with completed research state.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Current parent and both child continuity blocks were reread against current implementation summaries.
findingDetails.claim: Canonical spec continuity still directs consumers to repeat completed research.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Higher-precedence implementation summaries and the parent phase map correctly report completion.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: Summary-only consumers avoid the stale fields, but canonical spec/index readers can still observe them.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Prove all relevant consumers ignore spec continuity after an implementation summary exists.

findingDetails.title: Dependency graph metadata omits every declared implementation prerequisite
findingDetails.evidence: Phase 005-010 metadata arrays remain empty while current specs/plans declare ordered prerequisites.
findingDetails.recommendation: Populate canonical machine-readable dependency edges from the declared build order.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Every implementation-phase metadata file 005-010 was reread beside its dependency section.
findingDetails.claim: Machine-readable graph metadata still cannot represent the packet's build order.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Human-readable phase map and dependency sections preserve order for maintainers.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: The arrays may require explicit authoring, but remain the machine dependency surface.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Identify another canonical machine dependency source and prove consumers do not use manual.depends_on.

findingDetails.title: Hydration has no path-containment or symlink safety contract
findingDetails.evidence: Hash, generation, include, and byte-cap controls remain without canonical-root or symlink behavior.
findingDetails.recommendation: Specify root containment, traversal rejection, symlink policy, and adversarial tests.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Hydration requirements, ADR, reliability/security clauses, and checklist guard matrix were reread.
findingDetails.claim: Planned hydration can follow a selected artifact path outside the corpus root because containment is unspecified.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Generation hashing, artifact rehashing, scan bounds, and checklist guards.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: Integrity controls reduce drift but do not authorize resolved paths.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Show an incorporated parser contract with canonical containment and symlink rejection.

findingDetails.title: Unknown-rights styles are both forbidden from results and permitted as decision references
findingDetails.evidence: ADR result-membership language still conflicts with spec reference eligibility.
findingDetails.recommendation: Define typed reference-only rights semantics and reconcile ADR, requirements, and tests.
findingDetails.scopeProof: ADR membership, eligibility, hydration, failure scenario, and checklist clauses were reread together.
findingDetails.claim: Rights-unknown styles must never enter results yet may remain eligible as rationale references.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: Exact reuse and abstract evidence may be intended as separate states, but no typed split exists.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Specify reference-only state, prohibit raw hydration, reconcile ADR, and add tests.

findingDetails.title: The STUDY injection envelope has no malicious-instruction handling or test oracle
findingDetails.evidence: De-literalization and leak fixtures remain without malicious-instruction semantics or fail-closed oracle.
findingDetails.recommendation: Treat corpus observations as inert untrusted data and add seeded instruction fixtures.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Full STUDY requirement, security/reliability clauses, edge cases, and fixture matrix were reread.
findingDetails.claim: The STUDY prompt boundary still does not define or test malicious instructions embedded in corpus text.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: De-literalization, provenance refusal, no-raw-few-shot, leak gates, and adversarial fixtures.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: Those controls reduce literal copying but not semantically preserved adversarial instructions.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Specify inert-data handling and seeded prompt-injection fixtures with a fail-closed oracle.

findingDetails.title: Canonical research-output pointers bypass the actual lineage-owned syntheses
findingDetails.evidence: Four canonical pointers remain unlineaged and no unlineaged research.md aliases exist.
findingDetails.recommendation: Point to lineage-owned syntheses or publish verified stable aliases.
findingDetails.scopeProof: All four pointers and all three research roots were checked directly.
findingDetails.claim: Canonical output pointers still resolve to nonexistent unlineaged paths.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: All three research roots and implementation evidence naming lineage-owned outputs.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: The pointers predate lineage placement, but no resolver or alias exists.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Prove a supported resolver or add verified stable aliases.

findingDetails.title: The shared cross-mode contract has no concrete package owner or explicit phase-004 proof mapping
findingDetails.evidence: Phase 007 remains pathless while phase 004 is concrete and phases 008-010 consume the seam.
findingDetails.recommendation: Name the canonical package and document the phase-004 proof mapping before implementation.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Concrete producer, proposed owner, and all named consumer contracts were reread; no implementation failure exists.
findingDetails.classificationRationale: Advisory remains appropriate because the issue is pre-implementation ownership ambiguity and consumers prohibit local redefinition.
